query: What is the capacity threshold that triggers the large generation interconnection process in NYISO according to the NYISO process manual 23 and guide UG21? Cite the relevant source report and section you got this from
final_answer:
I have all the information I need to answer the question thoroughly.

# Capacity Threshold Triggering the NYISO Large Generation Interconnection Process

## The Answer: 20 MW

**The capacity threshold that triggers the NYISO Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP) is 20 MW** (i.e., the Large Facility process applies to generating facilities that **exceed 20 MW**; facilities 20 MW and below fall under the Small Generator Interconnection Procedures — SGIP).

## Source Citations

### 1) NYISO Manual 23 — Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual (TEI Manual)
- **Document:** NYISO Manual 23, "Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual"
- **Current Version:** 4.3, Effective Date 02/07/2025 (Source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/2924447/M-23-TEI-v4.2-Memo-Final.pdf)
- **Section:** Section 3 — *Interconnection Process*, with the LFIP covered specifically in **§3.3 Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)**, including:
  - **§3.3.1 Basic Information about the LFIP** (begins on p. 28)
  - **§3.3.2 Large Facility Interconnection Request** (begins on p. 32)
  - **§3.3.3 Basic Steps of the LFIP** (begins on p. 33)
- Manual 23's Table of Contents confirms these sections, and the manual explains how projects above the 20 MW threshold flow through the Large Facility process. Note: As of May 1, 2024 (Docket No. ER24-1915), NYISO filed new Standard Interconnection Procedures in OATT Attachment HH under FERC Order No. 2023, which is replacing the LFIP/SGIP framework — Section 3 only continues to apply to legacy Class Year 2023 projects and load interconnections per the transition rules.

### 2) NYISO Guide UG-21 — Transmission Expansion and Interconnection User's Guide
- **Document:** NYISO UG-21, "Transmission Expansion and Interconnection User's Guide"
- **Current Version:** 3.0, Issued July 2025, Effective Date 07/18/2025 (Source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf)
- **Section:** **§6 — What is the study process for generation interconnection projects?** (p. 2) — describes the Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP) and its applicability, including the requirement for "Attestations (for Generating Facilities greater than 20 MW) required by the final, approved NYSRC Reliability Rule B.5 (RR 151) establishing minimum interconnection standards for Inverter Based Resource (IBR) Generating Facilities based on IEEE Standard 2800-2022."
- The guide also references the underlying tariff authority: **OATT Attachment HH – Standard Interconnection Procedures for Generating Facilities, Cluster Study Transmission Projects and projects requesting CRIS**.

### 3) Supporting Source: NYISO Open Access Transmission Tariff (OATT)
- **Document:** NYISO OATT, Section 30 / Attachment X — Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (Source: https://nyisoviewer.etariff.biz/viewerdoclibrary/temp/71961cdc-6da5-445e-b75b-c2ae8294e04c.pdf)
- **Header of Attachment X:** *"30 Attachment X – Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (Applicable to Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs and to Class Year Transmission Facilities)"*
- **Section 30.3.1 (General):** States that the requirement to submit an Interconnection Request under Attachment X applies to "increases in capacity that results in total output in excess of 20 MW."

### 4) Corroborating Independent Source
- **NYSERDA Energy Storage Interconnection Guide** (https://www.nyserda.ny.gov/-/media/Project/Nyserda/Files/Programs/Energy-Storage/energy-storage-interconnection-guide.pdf) summarizes the threshold table:
  - "NYISO SGIP (Small Generator Interconnection Procedures): **≤20 MW**, NYISO connected"
  - "NYISO LFIP (Large Facility Interconnection Procedures): **>20 MW**, NYISO connected"

## Summary
Per **Manual 23 §3.3 (Large Facility Interconnection Procedures)** and **Guide UG-21 §6**, combined with the tariff language in **OATT Attachment X (Section 30)**, the **20 MW** threshold is what triggers the NYISO Large Facility (now superseded by Standard Interconnection Procedures in Attachment HH) interconnection process — any generating facility exceeding 20 MW, or any existing facility with a material capacity increase that would result in total output in excess of 20 MW, must enter the large-generation interconnection queue.
